texto: Only the king that has succeeded in subduing his senses is competent to resist his foes.
explicacao: Citação de Meera Uberoi sobre temas ligados a lideranca. “Only the king that has succeeded in subduing his senses is competent to resist his foes.” articula lideranca, leadership, wisdom-quotes em registro de lideranca, com relevância para contextos como wisdom-quotes.

texto: Readiness for action is the root of all kingly duties. Listen to the verse sung by Vrihaspati: By exertion the amrita was obtained, by exertion the asuras were slain and by exertion Indra obtained sovereignity in heaven and on earth. The heroes of exertion are superior to the heroes of speech. The heroes of speech gratify the heroes of exertion.

frase_original: Readiness for action is the root of all kingly duties. Listen to the verse sung by Vrihaspati: By exertion the amrita was obtained, by exertion the asuras were slain and by exertion Indra obtained sovereignity in heaven and on earth. The heroes of exertion are superior to the heroes of speech. The heroes of speech gratify the heroes of exertion.

texto: The king should act with promptitude, for without promptitude of action mere destiny never accomplishes the objects cherished by kings. The question for whether it is the king that makes the age or the age that makes the king, the king should entertain no doubt. It is the king that makes the age.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Meera Uberoi sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “The king should act with promptitude, for without promptitude of action mere destiny never accomp…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a lideranca, leadership, victory, útil em momentos de victory.
